## Description

Improve prose clarity using Strunk's principles and AI anti-pattern detection. Identifies LLM regression patterns and applies active voice, concrete language, and omit-needless-words principles.

## When to Use

- Editing documentation for clarity
- Cutting wordiness from explanations
- Removing AI-generated puffery
- Improving commit messages
- Improving UI copy
- Humanizing technical writing

## When NOT to Use

- Code documentation (use code-docs)
- Real-time writing
- Non-prose content
- Simple text files

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Identify Issues

- Scan text for AI patterns
- Note wordiness
- Identify vague language
- Flag promotional adjectives

### Phase 2: Analyze Patterns

- Categorize issues
- Assess impact
- Prioritize fixes
- Plan rewrites

### Phase 3: Rewrite

- Use active voice
- Replace vague with concrete
- Remove unnecessary words
- Simplify language

### Phase 4: Verify

- Read revised text
- Confirm clarity
- Check for remaining issues
- Get feedback

## Tools & References

- **Related Skills**: humanizer
- **Strunk's Elements**: Active voice, concrete language
- **AI Patterns**: Puffery, empty phrases, promotional adjectives
- **Vocabulary**: Leverage, multifaceted, pivotal, crucial

## Best Practices

- Use specific, concrete language
- Prefer active voice
- Remove unnecessary words
- Avoid promotional adjectives
- Vary sentence structure
- Read text aloud
- Get human feedback

## Skills Required

| Skill | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `humanizer` | Remove AI-isms and add natural voice |
| `content-research-writer` | Research and cite sources for content |

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] AI patterns and wordiness identified
- [ ] Issues categorized and prioritized
- [ ] Active voice applied throughout
- [ ] Vague language replaced with concrete terms
- [ ] Unnecessary words removed
- [ ] Revised text reviewed for clarity

## Pitfalls

- Over-editing can strip useful nuance from technical content
- Removing all adjectives makes text feel robotic
- Not reading aloud misses awkward phrasing
- Skipping human feedback leaves blind spots
